title: UI Synchronization (UI Slower than Code)
context: Mobile apps have transitions, animations, and network calls that make the UI render slower than Robot Framework executes. Without explicit waits, the script tries to interact with elements that are not yet ready, causing non-deterministic failures that are hard to reproduce.

problem.description: Robot Framework code executes faster than the mobile UI can render, causing interactions on elements that are not yet ready.
symptoms: ElementNotInteractableException on buttons that visually appear on screen
Test passes locally but fails on CI (slower device)

antipattern.description: Using Sleep 5s as a fixed delay
antipattern.why_bad: Makes the entire suite slow and still doesn't guarantee success on slower devices

solution.description: Always follow three steps: Wait for the element to be ready, perform the action, then verify the result.

steps.name: Wait Until Clickable
steps.description: Never use Click Element directly on a new screen. Always wait for the element to be present and visible first.
code: Wait Until Page Contains Element    ${SUBMIT_BTN}    timeout=10s

steps.name: Post-Action Verification
steps.description: After clicking something that changes state, verify the transition happened. This also acts as an implicit wait for the next screen.
code: Wait Until Page Does Not Contain Element    ${LOGIN_BTN}    # Confirm page switched

steps.name: Handle Animations
steps.description: For apps with heavy animations (sliding menus, loading spinners), wait for a stable landmark element rather than a fixed sleep.
code: # Wait for a known element that only appears after animation completes
Wait Until Page Contains    Welcome, User    timeout=5s

ai_instruction: When generating Robot scripts, NEVER use Sleep. Always generate a Wait Until... keyword before any interaction (Click, Input, Swipe). If a transaction is expected to be slow (e.g., network call, file upload), explicitly set a longer timeout argument (e.g., timeout=30s).
